preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 65%, a substantial margin over New Zealand's 11% win probability. Belgium enters as a clear favorite, though the model assigns the underdog a non-negligible chance rather than dismissing it outright.

The 24% draw probability indicates moderate competitive separation. While Belgium is preferred, the outcome space remains reasonably dispersed: roughly two-thirds of the model's probability mass clusters around a Belgium result, but one-quarter points to a stalemate. This suggests the match carries enough uncertainty to avoid appearing predetermined, though a New Zealand upset remains a low-probability tail event.

Model and market diverge meaningfully on all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus pushes Belgium's win probability to 82%, a gap of 17 percentage points above the model, while compressing both the draw (down 12pp) and New Zealand win (down 5pp) substantially. Polymarket odds align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures the true likelihood.
